baseline.notes: Both tasks failed has-svg and bar-count/has-path tests. D3 generates SVG dynamically via JavaScript but tests check static HTML for literal <svg>, <rect>, <path> tags.

iterations.changes: Rewrote skill to emphasize static SVG tags in HTML instead of D3 DOM manipulation. Added bar chart and line chart templates with static elements.
Added Node.js generator script pattern for large datasets (>10 rows). Model kept rewriting/failing the generator script for line chart.

Switched line chart to hybrid approach: static <svg> and <path> tags in HTML with D3 updating attributes at runtime. Model had persistent tool call formatting errors (raw JSON format) preventing file reads.
Made skill ultra-concise to reduce system prompt size and improve model tool call reliability. Same tool call formatting issue persisted for line chart.
Minimized skill to absolute minimum. Line chart finally succeeded. Bar chart regressed because skill lacked detailed static rect guidance, causing model to revert to dynamic D3 rect generation.

keyFindings: Tests check static HTML source for literal SVG tags — D3's dynamic DOM manipulation (append/join) creates elements at runtime that don't appear in the file
qwen3-30b intermittently generates malformed tool call JSON (wrapped in 'raw' field with trailing whitespace), causing all tool operations to fail
This tool call formatting issue appears random — sometimes bar-chart task is affected, sometimes line-chart, making consistent 100/100 unreliable
Shorter skill content correlates with better model behavior on the line chart task (90 data points)
Bar chart needs explicit static <rect> template guidance; without it the model reverts to dynamic D3 patterns
The optimal skill length is a trade-off: too long causes tool call failures on complex tasks, too short lacks guidance for simpler tasks
